summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the Twig sandbox configuration used by the Formulas service permits an unbounded for-range loop formula that exhausts CPU resources, demonstrating the denial-of-service vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the malicious Twig formula '{% for i in range(1, 999999999) %}{% endfor %}' to trigger unbounded CPU consumption inside the sandbox, exceeding a 30-second timeout and confirming the DoS condition.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, set up the Twig sandbox with the same policy as the Formulas service, and executed the malicious formula. The formula execution exceeded the 30-second timeout threshold, confirming that an attacker can cause sustained CPU consumption and deny service to other users.
verification_method: The verify.sh script runs both a benign formula (quick completion) and the malicious formula with a 30-second timeout. It confirms that the benign formula completes in under 2 seconds while the malicious formula is still running after 30 seconds. This distinguishes a genuine DoS condition from a startup failure or unrelated error.
evidence_produced_by_run: The benign Twig formula completed successfully within the expected timeframe, confirming the sandbox is functional.
The malicious formula with range(1, 999999999) exceeded the 30-second timeout, confirming unbounded resource consumption.
The process had to be killed via the timeout command, confirming the DoS condition persists until external intervention.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
